definition: The money that is available to a person or organization and a plan of how it will be spent over a period of time.
examples: I have a limited budget.
The budget is a plan of how it will be spent over a period of time.

definition: Money that is given by the government or another organization for a particular purpose, e.g. education.
examples: My sister received a grant for her research project.
The government gives a grant to help students.

definition: Money that a bank lends and somebody borrows.
examples: I had to take out a loan to buy my new car.
Bank loans often have high interest rates.

definition: An amount of money that you pay for professional advice or services, e.g. to a lawyer.
examples: The lawyer charged a high fee for his services.
You have to pay a fee for professional advice.

definition: The money that you pay to travel by bus, plane, taxi, etc.
examples: The bus fare has gone up again.
He paid his taxi fare and left.

definition: Money paid as punishment for breaking a law or rule.
examples: I had to pay a fine for illegal parking.
The company was given a heavy fine for breaking environmental rules.

definition: One of a number of payments that are made regularly until something has been paid for.
examples: We are paying for the sofa in monthly instalments.
I’ve just paid the final instalment on my loan.

definition: Having a lot of money, property, or valuable possessions; wealthy.
examples: They live in an affluent neighborhood.
The country is becoming more affluent.

definition: Having a lot of money; very rich (often used in negative sentences).
examples: Let her pay, she's loaded!
His parents are not very loaded.

definition: Having very little money, especially for a short period of time.
examples: He was so hard up he couldn't afford to eat out at all.
I'm a bit hard up this month, so no cinema for me.
I'm always broke by the end of the month.
I can't go out tonight, I'm flat broke.
I need five bucks for the subway.
It'll cost you twenty bucks.
It only cost me five quid.
I haven't got a quid on me.

definition: To have enough money to be able to buy or do something.
examples: House prices are rising and people can't afford to buy a home.
I can't afford a new car right now.

definition: Declared in law unable to pay outstanding debts.
examples: A lot of small businesses went bankrupt during the recession.
The company was declared bankrupt last year.
